input_candidates.truth_value: Taylor lattice structure consisting of multiple slip bands; planar dislocation glide dominates. Slip band spacing varies among grains, indicating inhomogeneous deformation.
input_candidates.prediction_value: Taylor-lattice-type low energy dislocation structure (LEDS) formed by planar dislocation glide; slip bands with dislocation pile-ups at intersections; inhomogeneous deformation.

input_candidates.truth_value: Taylor lattice with average slip band spacing 231 nm; organized planar slip bands.
input_candidates.prediction_value: Taylor lattice with increased slip bands; average slip band spacing ~231 nm; planar dislocation glide dominates.

input_candidates.truth_value: Appearance of microbands (average width 166 nm) and nano-sized deformation twins (average thickness 5.1 nm). Slip bands refined to ~64 nm spacing; planar glide still dominant but cross slip may occur at intersections.
input_candidates.prediction_value: Microbands with average width ~166 nm; nano-deformation twins with average thickness ~5.1 nm and high internal dislocation density; single slip bands hardly distinguishable.

input_candidates.truth_value: Presence of twins (average thickness increased to 19.2 nm), shear bands consisting of ultrafine grains, and dislocation tangles (average size 0.49 μm). Grains elongated along RD.
input_candidates.prediction_value: Twins size increased to ~19.2 nm; extensive shear bands comprising ultrafine grains confirmed by diffraction ring pattern; larger localized strain concentration.

input_candidates.truth_value: Dislocation cell structure and shear bands; grains heavily elongated; ultrahigh strength achieved. Cross slip activation leads to wavy glide.
input_candidates.prediction_value: Dislocation tangles structure with average size ~0.49 μm; shear bands increased and eroded almost all matrix; grains visibly elongated along rolling direction.

input_candidates.truth_value: Stacking fault energy (SFE) of this steel estimated as ~108 mJ/m².
input_candidates.prediction_value: With cold rolling, the true stress at the necking point was similar at low to medium rolling reductions (<50%), while it sharply increased during further rolling.

input_candidates.truth_value: In the as-quenched state, uniform elongation exceeds 75%, demonstrating excellent work hardening capability.
input_candidates.prediction_value: The rolling texture transitioned from copper-type to brass-type after 50% cold rolling reduction, attributed to the combination of dislocation planar glide and deformation twinning.

input_candidates.truth_value: True stress at necking point is similar for low to medium reduction (<50%) but sharply enhanced at higher reductions.
input_candidates.prediction_value: Deformation twinning occurred during cold rolling despite a high stacking fault energy (~108 mJ/m2), explained by short range order (SRO) in the austenitic matrix and local stress concentration lowering the actual critical shear stress.

input_candidates.truth_value: Texture transition from copper-type to brass-type occurs at 50% rolling reduction, characterized by decrease of Cu and S components and increase of Brass and Goss.
input_candidates.prediction_value: The α-fiber texture intensity monotonously increased up to 90% rolling reduction, while the γ-fiber remained weak with slight strengthening at 90% reduction near the {111}〈110〉 E component.

input_candidates.truth_value: The presence of short range order (SRO) in austenitic matrix and inhomogeneous local stress concentration may reduce the critical twinning stress enabling twin formation despite high SFE.
input_candidates.prediction_value: Along the β-fiber, the Cu orientation increased first and then decreased, the S orientation behaved similarly but decreased later, and the Goss orientation monotonously increased, indicating a continuous shift from Brass towards Goss with rolling reduction.

input_candidates.truth_value: Dislocation cross slip becomes prominent at 90% reduction, leading to dislocation cell structure and wavy glide.
input_candidates.prediction_value: The deformation substructure evolution sequence with increasing rolling reduction was Taylor lattice → microbands → nano-twins → shear bands → dislocation cells, indicating a change from planar to wavy dislocation glide.
